narrative:
The heat dome has fractured as a weak trough passes to our north this weekend. The core of the heat retreats to the Atlantic (the Bermuda High) and builds anew over the Rockies. A front approaches from the north, reaching us Sunday. Today offers plenty of pleasant weather despite clouds, but scattered showers and thunderstorms develop this afternoon and increase in coverage and intensity late today, continuing into this evening. We'll pull for a lull in the action for a while Saturday, but be alert for widely scattered convection during the PM hours. Then showers and thunderstorms develop Sunday across the whole region. Our weather turns cooler early next week.

Unsettled Weather Ahead; Maybe Salvage Saturday

Last Updated Fri July 10 at 11:00AM by Ray Russell

The heat dome has fractured as a weak trough passes to our north this weekend. The core of the heat retreats to the Atlantic (the Bermuda High) and builds anew over the Rockies. A front approaches from the north, reaching us Sunday. Today offers plenty of pleasant weather despite clouds, but scattered showers and thunderstorms develop this afternoon and increase in coverage and intensity late today, continuing into this evening. We'll pull for a lull in the action for a while Saturday, but be alert for widely scattered convection during the PM hours. Then showers and thunderstorms develop Sunday across the whole region. Our weather turns cooler early next week.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar

The 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar is here and being delivered to our retail outlets. Thanks for a record-breaking pre-publication sale! You can still avoid shipping costs with our pickup events beginning Thursday, July 2, in Boone. We'll take that to Banner Elk, West Jefferson, Spruce Pine, and Black Mountain next week; click here for details. You can see all the winning photography at our Photo Contest page . Forecast Discussion

A very warm ridge is in the Atlantic and is migrating west toward the Rockies. A weak trough passes to our north this weekend and will drive a front through our region Sunday.

Unsettled weather results, and timing issues offer considerable uncertainty. I think the most active period today will be late this afternoon and evening; however, areas along the western escarpment will probably have scattered showers and thunderstorms earlier in the afternoon.

A lull in the action is possible for a while Saturday, with widely scattered showers and storms possible in the afternoon. Temperatures are seasonal, not hot.

As the front approaches and passes through Sunday, widespread showers and thunderstorms break out. Storms taper off by evening.

Monday and Tuesday, the front is to our south with low pressure developing in the Mid-Mississippi Valley. Cool air aloft sets us up for periods of rain and thundershowers, especially south of Watauga County, but areas to the north are not immune from lighter rain and thunderstorms. Temperatures will be impressively cooler.

Warmer air returns from the southwest late Tuesday. With the remnants of the cool pool aloft, I cannot rule out an afternoon thundershower.

In the second half of next week, warmer air gradually presses in from the west, and the front fades off the map. Wednesday looks dry, but I cannot rule out an afternoon thunderstorm Thursday.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Monday Message for July 6, 2026

It's the first Monday Message of July! Mercifully, temperatures are cooler this week, although rainfall continues to be scattered and somewhat unreliable. We also take a look back at June rainfall and temperatures.
